by_judge.judge: PRADEEP SINGH YERUR IS NOT HOLDING SITTING ON 04.06.2026.

narrative:
On June 4, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 46.2%, resolving 1,189 out of 2,572 scheduled cases across 44 judges. This represents a downward trend in clearance efficiency compared to earlier in the week, following hearing rates of 51.1% on June 3 (1,192 heard of 2,333 scheduled), 56.3% on June 2 (1,367 heard of 2,429 scheduled), and 52.3% on June 1 (1,115 heard of 2,133 scheduled). This dip in the hearing rate occurred alongside a notable expansion in the day's scheduled caseload, which was the highest of the four-day period.

Individual judge workloads and performance highlights varied significantly. S. Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19 managed the highest single-list load of 271 scheduled cases, achieving a 31.7% hearing rate with 86 cases heard. In contrast, H.P. Sandesh in Hall 8 demonstrated high efficiency, hearing 55 of 67 scheduled cases (82.1%), while R. Nataraj in Hall 12 heard 51 of 67 cases (76.1%). Justice Pradeep Singh Yerur did not hold court, leaving 17 scheduled cases unheard.

In terms of procedural stages, "Orders" remained the most heavily scheduled category with 1,069 cases, converting at a 47.8% rate (511 heard). Meanwhile, "Final Hearing" cases lagged behind with a conversion rate of 29.5% (136 heard of 461). Among case types, Writ Petitions (WP) dominated the roster with 1,002 scheduled and 427 heard (42.6%), followed by Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) at 41.5% (189 heard of 455). Criminal Revision Petitions (CRL.RP) achieved the highest clearance efficiency among major categories, resolving 86.2% of its scheduled workload (50 heard of 58).
